title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
summary: A look at OpenAI's new Lockdown Mode, an AI startup predicting hit films, and the evolving landscape of AI safety and trust.
imageAlt: Image from source article for OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
imageCaption: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
imageSourceTitle: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI brief. Today, we're covering some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. I've got my eyes on OpenAI's new security feature and an AI startup that's trying to predict the next big hit film.

OpenAI has just unveiled a new feature called Lockdown Mode, designed to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks. For those who might not know, prompt injection attacks involve hackers manipulating AI models by injecting malicious prompts. This new mode is a significant step forward in safeguarding sensitive information and preventing these types of attacks. It's a bit like adding a secure lock to your digital door – it's an extra layer of protection that can give users more peace of mind.

Next, I came across an AI startup called Quilty, which claims it can predict whether a script will make a hit film. Using AI to forecast box office success is an intriguing idea, and Quilty seems to be making some waves in the entertainment industry. I wonder if they'll be able to accurately predict the next Oscar winner.

Also on the board is the ongoing conversation about AI safety and trust. As AI models become more advanced and integrated into our daily lives, ensuring they're secure and reliable is crucial. OpenAI's Lockdown Mode is a step in the right direction, but it's clear that there's still more work to be done in this area.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
